nodes.title: [2023-02-27] Existentialist literature emerged from the philosophical movement of existentialism in mid-20th century Europe
nodes.description: Existentialist literature was a product of the philosophical movement known as existentialism, which emerged in the mid-20th century in Europe.

nodes.title: [2023-02-27] Existentialism emphasizes subjective experience and individual choice in understanding human existence
nodes.description: The movement was founded on the belief that traditional philosophy, with its emphasis on rationality and objective truth, was inadequate to address the core questions of human existence. Existentialists sought to develop a new understanding of the human condition that was rooted in subjective experience, individual choice, and the angst or dread that arises from the recognition of the human condition.

nodes.title: [2023-02-27] Existentialist literature explores themes of alienation, isolation, and anxiety
nodes.description: Existentialist literature reflected these ideas by exploring human experiences of alienation, isolation, and anxiety.

nodes.title: [2023-02-27] Existentialist writers emphasize individual agency and self-awareness
nodes.description: These writers believed that human beings were fundamentally free to make choices, but also recognized that this freedom came with an immense responsibility. They emphasized the importance of individual agency and self-awareness.

nodes.title: [2023-02-27] Existentialism encourages individuals to create their own meaning in life
nodes.description: Existentialists believe that the search for objective truth and external meaning can be a form of escape from the realities of human existence. Instead, they encourage individuals to embrace the freedom and responsibility that comes with creating their own meaning and purpose in life.

nodes.title: [2023-02-27] Existentialism promotes living authentically and taking responsibility for choices
nodes.description: Existentialism can have practical applications in your everyday life by encouraging you to live authentically and take responsibility for your choices and decisions.
